status: bounded sensitivity reference; not a full Dingrong source reproduction

source_anchor.citation: Qian Dingrong et al., Solid State Communications 56, 813-816 (1985)
source_anchor.source_interpretation: degenerate edge described with a Burstein-Moss construction; below-gap absorption includes free-carrier scattering
blocking_items: complete source equation and parameter transcription

declared_sensitivity_parameters.parameter_status: illustrative and not inferred from the Dingrong specimen

five_density_identifiability_design.interpretation: five distinct densities restore local rank for five positive log-parameters, but the weakest direction is over four orders of magnitude below the strongest

authorized_conclusions: At the Dingrong density scale, a parabolic conduction-band filling estimate can be wrong by an amount comparable to or larger than a narrow HgCdTe gap.
Burstein-Moss filling, valence recoil, band-gap renormalization, and observation shifts must remain separate terms.
One optical edge at one density has Jacobian rank one and cannot identify the five declared material parameters.
A density series can restore local rank but can remain severely ill-conditioned.
unauthorized_conclusions: The illustrative masses, nonparabolicity, gap, or renormalization coefficient are not inferred for the Dingrong specimen.
The illustrative optical edge is not a reproduction of a source data point.
The one-specimen correction is not transferred to low-density detector material.
The model does not yet include finite-temperature carrier statistics or free-carrier absorption.
